question: Define the Bolzano-Weierstrass property for a metric space.
expectedPoints: A metric space (X, d) has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property if every infinite subset of X has a limit point in X.

question: Explain the relationship between compactness and the Bolzano-Weierstrass property in a metric space.
expectedPoints: In a metric space (X, d), compactness is equivalent to the Bolzano-Weierstrass property.
This is a fundamental theorem which states: 'A metric space is compact if and only if every infinite subset has a limit point.'
This equivalence is a key reason why the Bolzano-Weierstrass property is a crucial characterization of compactness in metric spaces, distinguishing them from more general topological spaces.

question: Prove that a compact metric space (X, d) has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property.
expectedPoints: Assume, for contradiction, that there exists an infinite subset A of X with no limit point in X.
This implies that every point x in X has an open neighborhood U_x that contains at most one point of A (other than x itself if x is in A).
The collection {U_x : x in X} is an open cover of X.
By compactness, there exists a finite subcover {U_{x1}, U_{x2}, ..., U_{xn}}.
Since each U_{xi} contains at most one point of the infinite set A, the finite union of these sets can contain only finitely many points of A, which is a contradiction.
Therefore, every infinite subset A must have a limit point in X.
